title: Sort Patient Queue by Urgency and Arrival Time
description: The system shall sort the patient queue first by urgency level (lowest number first) and then by arrival time (earliest first).
rationale: This requirement ensures that patients with the highest urgency are seen first, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of triage operations.
business_value: Improves patient care outcomes by ensuring critical patients receive timely attention.

source.description: As a triage nurse I need the queue to be sorted first by urgency level (lowest number first) and then by arrival time (earliest first) so that the most critical patients are seen first.

acceptance_criteria: patients are ordered by urgency level ascending (lowest number first)
for patients with the same urgency level, they are ordered by arrival time ascending (earliest first)
postconditions: the patient queue is sorted according to the specified criteria
functional_constraints: sorting must be done in real-time or near-real-time for immediate effect
all patients must be included in the sorting process

non_functional_requirements.performance: sorting should complete within 10 seconds for a queue of up to 500 patients
non_functional_requirements.availability: the system must be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: the system must support an increase in patient volume by 50% without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: error messages should clearly indicate issues with sorting and provide guidance on how to resolve them

edge_cases: What if there are no patients in the queue?
What happens when a patient's urgency level is null or undefined?
How should the system handle extremely high patient volumes?
What if two patients have the same urgency level and arrival time?

interfaces.name: Patient Queue Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the database to retrieve and sort patient data.

test_cases.description: Verify sorting by urgency level ascending.
test_cases.expected_result: Patients are sorted based on their urgency levels in ascending order (lowest number first).

test_cases.description: Verify secondary sorting by arrival time for patients with the same urgency level.
test_cases.expected_result: For patients with identical urgency levels, they are further sorted by their arrival times in ascending order (earliest first).

test_cases.description: Test behavior when a patient's urgency level is null or undefined.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should handle missing or invalid urgency levels gracefully and not crash.
